name: Vulnerability Management — Tenable + Qualys + Wiz
description: ATT&CK technique coverage for vulnerability management and CSPM vendors: Tenable, Qualys, and Wiz. These tools primarily provide 'Identify' coverage — they detect exploitable conditions before attackers can use them. Coverage via NIST 800-53 controls RA-5, CM-8, SA-11, SI-2. Source: https://github.com/TeamStarWolf/TeamStarWolf/blob/main/data/vendor_to_technique.jsonl

techniques.comment: Tenable + Qualys + Wiz — RA-5: continuous scanning identifies exploitable public-facing vulnerabilities before attackers; reduces exploit window
Tenable + Qualys + Wiz — RA-5: exploitation of remote services prevented by identifying and remediating unpatched services
Tenable + Qualys + Wiz — RA-5/CM-8: local privilege escalation vulnerabilities identified and remediated before exploitation
Tenable + Qualys — RA-5: client-side exploitation vulnerabilities (browser, Office) identified via authenticated scanning
Wiz — CM-8: software supply chain vulnerabilities in container images and dependencies identified via CSPM/SCA scanning
Wiz + Qualys — Software Supply Chain compromise risk identified via SCA and container image scanning
Wiz — SA-11: implant in cloud image detected via cloud workload scanning and image integrity checks
Wiz — CM-8: container escape vulnerability identification in misconfigurations and CVEs
Wiz — RA-5: exposed credentials in cloud storage, environment variables, and metadata services identified via CSPM
Wiz — Credentials in Files: cloud storage and filesystem credential exposure detection
Wiz — Cloud Instance Metadata API: IMDS exposure and misconfigurations identified
Wiz + Tenable — RA-5/CM-8: cloud infrastructure discovery attack surface identified and reduced via asset inventory
Wiz — CM-8: cloud storage object discovery risks mitigated via bucket permission scanning
Wiz — AC-2/CM-8: over-privileged cloud accounts and service accounts identified via CIEM
Wiz — Additional Cloud Credentials: service account key exposure identified
